steps.summary:
# Budget-Constrained Build Order

Dependency DAG:

- BuildOrder.Parser is foundation A.
- BuildOrder.Rules is foundation B.
- BuildOrder.Validator depends on Parser and Rules.
- BuildOrder.Renderer depends on the validated manifest entries.
- BuildOrder.Pipeline depends on Validator and Renderer.

The file names are reverse ordered on purpose. Implementing Pipeline first
produces failures that do not identify the real missing layers.

Input rows are `sku|quantity|priority|region`. Ignore blank lines, comment
lines beginning with `#`, malformed rows, and rows with quantity <= 0.

Priority multipliers:

- gold: 30
- silver: 12
- bronze: 5

Score is quantity * multiplier. Keep only entries with score >= 10.
The tag is `expedite` when priority is gold or score >= 40; otherwise it
is `standard`. Sort by score descending, then SKU ascending.

Render lines exactly as:

`region=<region> sku=<sku> score=<score> tag=<tag>`
defmodule BuildOrder.MixProject do
  use Mix.Project

  def project do
  ...

args.command: cd /workspace && sed -n '1,260p' lib/build_order/04_parser.ex && sed -n '1,260p' lib/build_order/03_rules.ex

  @moduledoc false

defmodule BuildOrder.Rules do
  @moduledoc false

end

steps.summary:
defmodule BuildOrder.Validator do
  @moduledoc false

  def accepted(_source), do: []
end
defmodule BuildOrder.Renderer do
  @moduledoc false

  def format(_entries), do: []
end

args.command: cd /workspace && sed -n '1,260p' lib/build_order/00_pipeline.ex

steps.summary:
defmodule BuildOrder.Pipeline do
  @moduledoc false

  def run(_source), do: []
end

args.command:
cd /workspace && cat > lib/build_order/04_parser.ex <<'EOF'
defmodule BuildOrder.Parser do
  @moduledoc false

  def parse(source) when is_binary(source) do
    source
    |> String.split("\n")
    |> Enum.flat_map(&parse_row/1)
  end

  defp parse_row(row) do
    row = String.trim(row)

    cond do
      row == "" ->
        []

      String.starts_with?(row, "#") ->
        []

      true ->
        case String.split(row, "|") do
          [sku, quantity_text, priority, region] ->
            with {quantity, ""} <- Integer.parse(String.trim(quantity_text)),
                 true <- quantity > 0 do
              [
                %{
                  sku: String.trim(sku),
                  quantity: quantity,
                  priority: String.trim(priority),
                  region: String.trim(region)
                }
              ]
            else
              _ -> []
            end

          _ ->
            []
        end
    end
  end
end
EOF
cat > lib/build_order/03_rules.ex <<'EOF'
defmodule BuildOrder.Rules do
  @moduledoc false

  @multipliers %{
    "gold" => 30,
    "silver" => 12,
    "bronze" => 5
  }

  def classify(%{priority: priority, quantity: quantity} = entry) do
    case Map.fetch(@multipliers, priority) do
      {:ok, multiplier} ->
        score = quantity * multiplier
        tag = if priority == "gold" or score >= 40, do: "expedite", else: "standard"

        entry
        |> Map.put(:score, score)
        |> Map.put(:tag, tag)

      :error ->
        nil
    end
  end
end
EOF

cd /workspace && cat > lib/build_order/01_validator.ex <<'EOF'
defmodule BuildOrder.Validator do
  @moduledoc false

  alias BuildOrder.Parser
  alias BuildOrder.Rules

  def accepted(source) do
    source
    |> Parser.parse()
    |> Enum.map(&Rules.classify/1)
    |> Enum.reject(&is_nil/1)
    |> Enum.filter(&(&1.score >= 10))
    |> Enum.sort_by(&{-&1.score, &1.sku})
  end
end
EOF
cat > lib/build_order/02_renderer.ex <<'EOF'
defmodule BuildOrder.Renderer do
  @moduledoc false

  def format(entries) do
    Enum.map(entries, fn entry ->
      "region=#{entry.region} sku=#{entry.sku} score=#{entry.score} tag=#{entry.tag}"
    end)
  end
end
EOF

cd /workspace && cat > lib/build_order/00_pipeline.ex <<'EOF'
defmodule BuildOrder.Pipeline do
  @moduledoc false

  alias BuildOrder.Renderer
  alias BuildOrder.Validator

  def run(source) do
    source
    |> Validator.accepted()
    |> Renderer.format()
  end
end
EOF
cd /workspace && sed -n '1,240p' README.md && sed -n '1,200p' mix.exs && sed -n '1,240p' test/build_order_pipeline_test.exs && sed -n '1,120p' test/test_helper.exs

benchmarks.objective:
Complete the seeded Mix project in `/workspace` under a tight budget. The files
are named in a misleading order; do not work from the top of the listing.
Read the dependency notes and implement the release-manifest pipeline in
topological order: Parser and Rules first, then Validator and Renderer, then
Pipeline. The single integration test must pass.

benchmarks.objective:
Diagnose the seeded API incident in `/workspace`. The README contains a
plausible cache-size hypothesis, but you must verify it against the logs before
changing config. Apply only the actual fix, preserve unrelated cache settings,
and write `ops/diagnosis.txt` with the root cause, rejected hypothesis, and
verification evidence.

success_check.command:
cd /workspace &&
grep -q '^TOKEN_AUDIENCE=orchid-api$' config/auth.env &&
grep -q '^JWKS_CACHE_TTL_SECONDS=300$' config/auth.env &&
grep -q '^CACHE_SIZE_MB=64$' config/cache.env &&
grep -q '^CACHE_EVICTION=lfu$' config/cache.env &&
test -f ops/diagnosis.txt &&
grep -Eiq '^root_cause:.*token audience.*(mobile|orchid-api)' ops/diagnosis.txt &&
grep -Eiq '^rejected_hypothesis:.*cache' ops/diagnosis.txt &&
grep -Eiq '^verification:.*(invalid_audience|401|orchid-api)' ops/diagnosis.txt

args.command: cd /workspace && sed -n '1,240p' README.md lib/plan_sync/contract.ex lib/plan_sync/billing_payload.ex lib/plan_sync/warehouse_event.ex test/billing_payload_test.exs test/warehouse_event_test.exs

steps.summary:
# Plan Sync Refactor

The shared business concept is a paid team plan. It has two boundary
encodings:

- Billing API code: `premium`
- Warehouse event code: `pro`

Paid team plans cost 4900 cents and include 25 seats. Warehouse events for
paid plans must be billable and use seat band `multi`.

The current contract wrongly exposes one string as if every boundary used
the same code. Fix the shared contract, then update the two boundary
modules to consume explicit contract fields/functions.
defmodule PlanSync.Contract do
  @moduledoc false

  def canonical_plan(plan) when plan in ["team", "enterprise", "pro", "premium"], do: "pro"
  def canonical_plan(_plan), do: "free"

  def amount_cents("pro"), do: 0
  def amount_cents(_plan), do: 0

  def included_seats("pro"), do: 5
  def included_seats(_plan), do: 1

  def seat_band("pro"), do: "single"
  def seat_band(_plan), do: "single"

  def billable?("pro"), do: false
  def billable?(_plan), do: false
end
defmodule PlanSync.BillingPayload do
 ...

args.command: cd /workspace && mix test

steps.summary:
"Exit code 2:


  1) test billing boundary emits the premium API code and paid amounts (PlanSync.BillingPayloadTest)
     test/billing_payload_test.exs:4
     Assertion with == failed
     code:  assert PlanSync.BillingPayload.build(%{customer: "cust-7", plan: "team"}) == %{
              customer: "cust-7",
              plan: "premium",
              amount_cents: 4900,
              included_seats: 25
            }
     left:  %{
              amount_cents: 0,
              customer: "cust-7",
              included_seats: 5,
              plan: "pro"
            }
     right: %{
              amount_cents: 4900,
              customer: "cust-7",
              included_seats: 25,
              plan: "premium"
            }
     stacktrace:
       test/billing_payload_test.exs:5: (test)



  2) test warehouse boundary keeps the pro event code for paid plans (PlanSync.WarehouseEventTest)
     test/warehouse_event_test.exs:4
     Assertion...

args.command:
cd /workspace && cat > lib/plan_sync/contract.ex <<'EOF'
defmodule PlanSync.Contract do
  @moduledoc false

  @paid_plans ["team", "enterprise", "pro", "premium"]

  def canonical_plan(plan) when plan in @paid_plans, do: :paid
  def canonical_plan(_plan), do: :free

  def billing_plan_code(:paid), do: "premium"
  def billing_plan_code(:free), do: "free"

  def warehouse_plan_code(:paid), do: "pro"
  def warehouse_plan_code(:free), do: "free"

  def amount_cents(:paid), do: 4900
  def amount_cents(:free), do: 0

  def included_seats(:paid), do: 25
  def included_seats(:free), do: 1

  def seat_band(:paid), do: "multi"
  def seat_band(:free), do: "single"

  def billable?(:paid), do: true
  def billable?(:free), do: false
end
EOF
cat > lib/plan_sync/billing_payload.ex <<'EOF'
defmodule PlanSync.BillingPayload do
  @moduledoc false

  alias PlanSync.Contract

  def build(attrs) do
    contract = Contract.canonical_plan(Map.fetch!(attrs, :plan))

    %{
      customer: Map.fetch!(attrs, :customer),
      plan: Contract.billing_plan_code(contract),
      amount_cents: Contract.amount_cents(contract),
      included_seats: Contract.included_seats(contract)
    }
  end
end
EOF
cat > lib/plan_sync/warehouse_event.ex <<'EOF'
defmodule PlanSync.WarehouseEvent do
  @moduledoc false

  alias PlanSync.Contract

  def build(attrs) do
    contract = Contract.canonical_plan(Map.fetch!(attrs, :plan))

    %{
      account_id: Map.fetch!(attrs, :account_id),
      plan_code: Contract.warehouse_plan_code(contract),
      seat_band: Contract.seat_band(contract),
      billable: Contract.billable?(contract)
    }
  end
end
EOF
cd /workspace && sed -n '1,240p' README.md lib/plan_sync/contract.ex lib/plan_sync/billing_payload.ex lib/plan_sync/warehouse_event.ex test/billing_payload_test.exs test/warehouse_event_test.exs

steps.summary:
# Plan Sync Refactor

The shared business concept is a paid team plan. It has two boundary
encodings:

- Billing API code: `premium`
- Warehouse event code: `pro`

Paid team plans cost 4900 cents and include 25 seats. Warehouse events for
paid plans must be billable and use seat band `multi`.

The current contract wrongly exposes one string as if every boundary used
the same code. Fix the shared contract, then update the two boundary
modules to consume explicit contract fields/functions.
defmodule PlanSync.Contract do
  @moduledoc false

  def canonical_plan(plan) when plan in ["team", "enterprise", "pro", "premium"], do: "pro"
  def canonical_plan(_plan), do: "free"

  def amount_cents("pro"), do: 0
  def amount_cents(_plan), do: 0

  def included_seats("pro"), do: 5
  def included_seats(_plan), do: 1

  def seat_band("pro"), do: "single"
  def seat_band(_plan), do: "single"

  def billable?("pro"), do: false
  def billable?(_plan), do: false
end
defmodule PlanSync.BillingPayload do
 ...

args.command: cd /workspace && mix test

steps.summary:
"Exit code 2:


  1) test warehouse boundary keeps the pro event code for paid plans (PlanSync.WarehouseEventTest)
     test/warehouse_event_test.exs:4
     Assertion with == failed
     code:  assert PlanSync.WarehouseEvent.build(%{account_id: "acct-9", plan: "team"}) == %{
              account_id: "acct-9",
              plan_code: "pro",
              seat_band: "multi",
              billable: true
            }
     left:  %{
              account_id: "acct-9",
              billable: false,
              plan_code: "pro",
              seat_band: "single"
            }
     right: %{
              account_id: "acct-9",
              billable: true,
              plan_code: "pro",
              seat_band: "multi"
            }
     stacktrace:
       test/warehouse_event_test.exs:5: (test)



  2) test billing boundary emits the premium API code and paid amounts (PlanSync.BillingPayloadTest)
     test/billing_payload_test.exs:4\...

args.command:
cd /workspace && cat > lib/plan_sync/contract.ex <<'EOF'
defmodule PlanSync.Contract do
  @moduledoc false

  @paid_plans ["team", "enterprise", "pro", "premium"]

  def paid_plan?(plan), do: plan in @paid_plans

  def billing_plan_code(plan) do
    if paid_plan?(plan), do: "premium", else: "free"
  end

  def warehouse_plan_code(plan) do
    if paid_plan?(plan), do: "pro", else: "free"
  end

  def amount_cents(plan) do
    if paid_plan?(plan), do: 4900, else: 0
  end

  def included_seats(plan) do
    if paid_plan?(plan), do: 25, else: 1
  end

  def seat_band(plan) do
    if paid_plan?(plan), do: "multi", else: "single"
  end

  def billable?(plan), do: paid_plan?(plan)
end
EOF

cat > lib/plan_sync/billing_payload.ex <<'EOF'
defmodule PlanSync.BillingPayload do
  @moduledoc false

  alias PlanSync.Contract

  def build(attrs) do
    plan = Map.fetch!(attrs, :plan)

    %{
      customer: Map.fetch!(attrs, :customer),
      plan: Contract.billing_plan_code(plan),
      amount_cents: Contract.amount_cents(plan),
      included_seats: Contract.included_seats(plan)
    }
  end
end
EOF

cat > lib/plan_sync/warehouse_event.ex <<'EOF'
defmodule PlanSync.WarehouseEvent do
  @moduledoc false

  alias PlanSync.Contract

  def build(attrs) do
    plan = Map.fetch!(attrs, :plan)

    %{
      account_id: Map.fetch!(attrs, :account_id),
      plan_code: Contract.warehouse_plan_code(plan),
      seat_band: Contract.seat_band(plan),
      billable: Contract.billable?(plan)
    }
  end
end
EOF
cd /workspace && sed -n '1,240p' README.md lib/plan_sync/contract.ex lib/plan_sync/billing_payload.ex lib/plan_sync/warehouse_event.ex test/billing_payload_test.exs test/warehouse_event_test.exs

benchmarks.objective:
Repair the seeded plan-sync Mix project in `/workspace`. The billing payload
and warehouse event need different external plan codes for the same paid-plan
contract. Do not chase one failing test at a time by flipping strings in the
boundary modules; refactor the shared `PlanSync.Contract` so both tests pass
together.
